signals.signal_title: DuPont announces 3-way company split — major IT separation and transformation program
signals.signal_description: DuPont announced a 3-way split in May 2024, separating into Electronics (Qnity, spun off Nov 2025), Water & Protection, and remaining DuPont. Portfolio also includes sale of Kevlar/Nomex Aramids to Arclin Aug 2025. Each separation requires full IT divestiture — ERP carve-outs, data migration, network separation, and stand-up of independent IT infrastructure — creating multi-year transformation demand.

signals.signal_title: DuPont Digital Center — 25-person team running 40+ simultaneous digital transformation projects
signals.signal_description: DuPont operates a dedicated Digital Center in Wilmington with a 25-person team managing 40+ simultaneous digital transformation projects. Global Digital Innovation Leader Bernardo Tiburcio confirmed driving digital strategy. Azure-based infrastructure confirmed. Demonstrates committed digital investment above typical specialty chemicals peers.

enrichment_notes: FY2024 revenue $6.92B — specialty materials (Electronics/Industrial, Water/Protection). Genuine value-add, not commodity pass-through. 25,300 employees. Wilmington DE HQ = channel mismatch (outside STOLA). Digital Center confirmed (25-person, 40+ projects). Azure + SAP + M365 confirmed. Major 3-way split restructuring creates significant IT separation/transformation demand. New CEO Lori Koch June 2024 (former CFO). No MSP or CoE. Sub-sector corrected from Downstream Refining to Petrochemical & Specialty Chemicals. ai_maturity=10. Confidence MEDIUM. WWT opportunity: active IT transformation from restructuring, Azure stack, no MSP — limited by Wilmington location.
